title: Add the bidirectional enforcement-drift check and the invariant CLI
description:
Add a bidirectional enforcement-drift check and the `jit invariant` CLI surface. Drift is checked as declaration-consistency (not execution): in one direction, an invariant whose `enforced-by` names a missing or unloadable rule or gate; in the other, a rule or gate that no invariant claims. `jit invariant render` projects the registry and `jit invariant check` runs the drift check, each supporting `--json`; `jit invariant check` exits non-zero when drift is found.

The declaration-consistency framing follows from the confirmed fact that `Assertion::CheckerCommand` / `enforced-by` bindings are never executed: the local write path skips them (`crates/jit/src/validation/local.rs:288`) and validate has no execution site. A new `Assertion` variant is dispatched at `crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:355`; a new `commands/invariant.rs` is registered in `commands/mod.rs` with a thin `main.rs` dispatch arm. This task also corrects the stale docstring at `crates/jit/src/validation/local.rs:300` (which wrongly claims `checker-command` runs during validation).

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: Validate reports a declared-but-unenforced invariant (its `enforced-by` names a missing or unloadable rule/gate) as a finding.
- [hard] REQ-02: Validate reports an enforced-but-undeclared rule/gate (claimed by no invariant) as a finding.
- [hard] REQ-03: Both drift directions have explicit tests under `cargo test`.
- [hard] REQ-04: Running `jit invariant render` invokes the configured invariant projection and writes or updates the configured documentation target.
- [hard] REQ-05: `jit invariant render` and `jit invariant check` each support `--json`.
- [hard] REQ-06: `jit invariant check` exits non-zero when drift is present.
- [hard] REQ-07: The stale docstring at `validation/local.rs:300` is corrected to reflect that `checker-command` is not executed during validation.
